mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers atmospheric composition, the electromagnetic spectrum relevant to absorption and heating, greenhouse effect, and ocean-atmosphere interactions central to this entire section.
Addresses atmospheric structure, wind systems, cyclones, anticyclones, and El Nino, directly relevant to the large-scale atmospheric motions in B.1-B.7.
Analyzes atmospheric circulation patterns, jet streams, trade winds, and monsoon systems relevant to the tropical and mid-latitude wind systems in B.4-B.5.
Situates the carbon, nitrogen, and sulfur budgets of the atmosphere (A.1.a.ii-iv) within an integrated Earth system framework, including greenhouse processes.

Explains solar radiation and solar-terrestrial interactions -- flares, coronal mass ejections, and the solar wind -- directly relevant to ionospheric disturbances, auroras, and magnetic storms (A.3.b.ii).
Treats the ionosphere, Van Allen radiation belts (A.3.c.iii), and Earth's magnetic field interactions with the upper atmosphere.
Examines extreme weather storms including hurricanes and typhoons (B.5.b) and severe mid-latitude cyclonic disturbances (B.4.b).
Provides planetary context for the atmosphere's layered structure and its role in the water cycle (A.1.a.i) and global circulation patterns.
Explains electromagnetic radiation relevant to ultraviolet absorption by the ozonosphere (A.3.a.i) and the effects of the ionosphere on radio waves (A.3.b.iii).
Discusses atmospheric effects on observations including turbulence and scintillation, plus radio wave propagation relevant to ionospheric effects on radio waves (A.3.b.iii).
